{"key_event":"New Deal expansion of federal administrative state","reasoning":"The Depression weakened markets, local relief systems, and some rural communities, but the Wagner Act, CIO organizing, mass strikes, and New Deal electoral realignment modestly strengthened organized civil society by 1938. Federal banking control, relief and work programs, Social Security, labor regulation, and new agencies substantially expanded national administrative capacity, though courts, elections, federalism, and organized opposition kept the state below overwhelming power.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":8.0,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":8.9}
